hypothesis: If lattice-structure mechanical property data are arranged as a low-rank tensor and only limited or bias-skewed samples are observed, then tensor-completion surrogates (CPD-S/NeAT and their ensemble) will achieve higher R² in predicting Young’s modulus and mass-normalized modulus than Gaussian Process, XGBoost, and MLP baselines because the low-rank tensor structure captures inter-parameter correlations that enable robust recovery from incomplete or biased sampling.
